chapter_title.en: Chapter on What Becomes Permissible for the mutamatti' and the Mufrid After Slaughtering and Shaving Before Visiting the Sacred House

text: 3096 - وَ رَوَى عَلِيُّ بْنُ اَلنُّعْمَانِ عَنْ سَعِيدٍ اَلْأَعْرَجِ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ رَمَى اَلْجِمَارَ وَ ذَبَحَ وَ حَلَقَ رَأْسَهُ أَ يَلْبَسُ قَمِيصاً وَ قَلَنْسُوَةً قَبْلَ أَنْ يَزُورَ اَلْبَيْتَ فَقَالَ «إِنْ كَانَ مُتَمَتِّعاً فَلاَ وَ إِنْ كَانَ مُفْرِداً لِلْحَجِّ فَنَعَمْ».

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3096 - Ali ibn al-Nu'man narrated from Sa'id al-A'raj, from Abu Abdullah (as), who said:
I asked him about a man who has stoned the pillars, slaughtered his sacrifice, and shaved his head—can he wear a shirt and a cap before visiting the House?
Imam (as) replied: "If he is performing Tamattu' (Hajj combined with Umrah), then no. But if he is performing Ifrad (Hajj alone), then yes."
